title: U.S. Trade Court Blocks Second Round of Trump Tariffs
subtitle: A legal battle over global import taxes creates uncertainty for the administration's economic strategy.

summary.en: A U.S. trade court recently ruled that a second set of global tariffs imposed by President Trump is illegal. This decision follows a Supreme Court ruling that previously struck down an earlier, similar tax on imports. The administration had hoped to use a different law to keep the tariffs in place, but the court found that the necessary conditions for such taxes were not met. Importers who successfully challenged the policy are now set to receive refunds for the money they paid. The ruling creates new challenges for the president as he prepares for upcoming international trade negotiations.
summary.zh: 美國貿易法院近期裁定，川普總統實施的第二輪全球關稅屬於非法。此項決定是在最高法院先前推翻一項類似的進口稅後所做出的。政府原希望援引另一項法律來維持關稅，但法院認為該稅收所需的必要條件並未達成。成功挑戰該政策的進口商現在將獲得退款。這項裁決為總統在準備即將到來的國際貿易談判時，帶來了新的挑戰。

paragraphs.en: A U.S. court has delivered a major setback to the Trump administration’s trade policy. The Court of International Trade recently struck down a second round of global tariffs—taxes on goods brought into the country from abroad. This decision comes shortly after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that the president had gone beyond his legal power when he first ordered double-digit taxes on almost all products imported into the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 美國法院對川普 (Donald Trump) 政府的貿易政策造成了重大打擊。國際貿易法院近期推翻了第二輪全球關稅，即針對從國外進口至國內的商品所徵收的稅款。在此之前，美國最高法院才剛裁定，總統在首次下令對幾乎所有進口至美國的產品徵收雙位數稅率時，已經逾越了法律賦予的權限。

paragraphs.en: Following the Supreme Court’s decision in February, the administration attempted to replace the original taxes by using a different, older law. However, this law only allows the government to impose tariffs if there is a large and persistent balance-of-payments deficit. This is a specific economic term that describes a situation where a country spends more money on foreign goods and services than it earns from them. The trade court ruled that this specific condition does not currently exist, meaning the new tariffs were not legally allowed.
paragraphs.zh: 繼最高法院二月份做出裁決後，政府試圖援引一項不同的舊法來取代原有的稅收。然而，這項法律僅允許政府在出現龐大且持續的國際收支逆差時才能徵收關稅。這是一個特定的經濟術語，用來描述一個國家在購買外國商品與服務上的支出，超過了從中賺取的收入。貿易法院裁定，目前並不存在這種特定條件，這意味著這些新關稅在法律上是不被允許的。

paragraphs.en: According to NPR Business, the court’s decision was limited to the two companies that brought the case to court, along with the state of Washington. Because of this, it is not yet clear if other businesses will stop paying the tariffs or if they will also be able to get their money back. Jeffrey Schwab, a lawyer who represented the importers, noted that the situation is still confusing and will depend on what happens in the coming weeks. Meanwhile, the government is already preparing to refund more than $166 billion to businesses that paid the original, now-illegal emergency tariffs.
paragraphs.zh: 根據美國公共廣播電台 (NPR) 商業頻道的報導，法院的裁決僅限於提起訴訟的兩家公司以及華盛頓州。因此，目前尚不清楚其他企業是否會停止繳納關稅，或者是否也能夠取回款項。代表進口商的律師傑佛瑞·史瓦布 (Jeffrey Schwab) 指出，目前情況仍令人困惑，將取決於未來幾週的發展。與此同時，政府已準備好向那些支付了最初已被判定為非法之緊急關稅的企業，退還超過一千六百六十億美元的款項。

paragraphs.en: For many business owners, the court’s decision is a welcome relief. Jay Foreman, whose company imports toys, said he expects to receive about $7 million in refunds. Foreman argued that while it might make sense to put taxes on some specific, strategic products, a general 10 percent tax on everything from around the world is harmful to both companies and regular shoppers. He compared the administration’s broad approach to using a "bazooka" when a much smaller, more careful tool was needed.
paragraphs.zh: 對於許多企業主而言，法院的裁決是一項令人欣慰的解脫。經營玩具進口公司的傑·福爾曼 (Jay Foreman) 表示，他預計將收到約七百萬美元的退款。福爾曼認為，雖然對某些特定的戰略性產品徵稅或許有其道理，但對全球所有商品一律徵收百分之十的稅，對企業與一般消費者都有害。他將政府這種廣泛的手段比喻為在需要精細工具時，卻動用了「火箭筒」。

paragraphs.en: Ars Technica reports that this ruling leaves the president in a difficult position. His main trade strategy has been to use tariffs to force companies to move their manufacturing work back into the United States. With this latest legal defeat, he has fewer tools available to carry out that plan. This is especially important as he prepares for upcoming meetings with China’s President Xi Jinping. Analysts suggest that the president now has less power to negotiate, as he cannot easily use these tariffs as a way to pressure other nations.
paragraphs.zh: 科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導指出，這項裁決使總統陷入了困境。他主要的貿易策略是利用關稅迫使企業將製造業務遷回美國。隨著這次法律上的挫敗，他能用來執行該計畫的工具變得更少。這點尤為重要，因為他正準備與中國國家主席習近平進行會晤。分析師認為，總統現在的談判籌碼減少了，因為他無法再輕易利用這些關稅作為向其他國家施壓的手段。

paragraphs.en: Despite the court's ruling, the administration is not giving up. According to The New York Times, the president has criticized the judges and stated that he will look for other ways to continue his tariff agenda using different laws. Experts believe that finding new legal paths could take weeks or even months. In the meantime, there is a possibility that more companies will file lawsuits to demand refunds, especially if they can prove that the tariffs forced them to raise prices for their own customers.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管法院做出了裁決，政府並未放棄。根據《紐約時報》(The New York Times) 報導，總統批評了法官，並表示將尋求其他途徑，利用不同的法律繼續推動其關稅議程。專家認為，尋找新的法律途徑可能需要數週甚至數月。在此期間，若更多企業能證明關稅迫使他們調漲售價，他們可能會提起訴訟要求退款。

paragraphs.en: While the administration plans to appeal the court’s decision, the immediate future of U.S. trade policy remains uncertain. The original tariffs were already set to expire in July, but the legal battles have added a layer of complexity for businesses that rely on international trade. For now, the government must focus on processing the massive amount of refunds while trying to find a new strategy that can survive a challenge in court. Whether the administration can find a way to keep its trade goals alive without breaking the law remains the central question for economists and business leaders alike.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然政府計畫對法院的裁決提出上訴，但美國貿易政策的近期前景仍充滿不確定性。原有的關稅本已預定於七月到期，但法律訴訟為依賴國際貿易的企業增添了複雜性。目前，政府必須專注於處理巨額的退款事宜，同時設法尋找一套能通過法院檢驗的新策略。政府能否在不違法的情況下實現其貿易目標，仍是經濟學家與企業領袖共同關注的核心問題。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To officially decide that a law or rule is no longer valid or legal.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （法院）判決某項法律或規定無效、廢除。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「擊倒」，這裡指法律上的廢除。文中：The Court of International Trade recently struck down a second round of global tariffs—taxes on goods brought into the country from abroad.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To perform or complete a task or plan.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 carry 和介詞 out 組成，意思與原意不同。文中：With this latest legal defeat, he has fewer tools available to carry out that plan.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A problem that delays or prevents progress.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 set 和 back 組成，指進展受阻。文中：A U.S. court has delivered a major setback to the Trump administration’s trade policy.

sources.name: NPR Business
sources.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Court rules Trump's 10% tariff is just as illegal as the tariff it replaced

quiz.question: What specific justification did the administration use to attempt to impose the second round of tariffs?

options.A: The need to protect domestic toy manufacturers from foreign competition.
options.B: The existence of a large and persistent balance-of-payments deficit.
options.C: The authority granted by the Supreme Court in their February ruling.
options.D: The requirement to pressure China before upcoming presidential meetings.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，政府試圖使用一項舊法律來實施關稅，而該法律的前提條件是國家必須存在「大規模且持續的國際收支逆差（balance-of-payments deficit）」。選項 A 是 Jay Foreman 的個人例子，而非法律依據；選項 C 錯誤，因為最高法院其實是裁定總統權力過當；選項 D 是關稅帶來的政治影響，而非實施關稅的法律理由。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the court's ruling on the president's upcoming diplomatic negotiations?

options.A: The president will likely cancel his meetings with President Xi Jinping.
options.B: The president will have more leverage because he can now focus on domestic issues.
options.C: The president's ability to use economic pressure on other nations has been weakened.
options.D: The president will be forced to accept all of China's trade demands.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，分析師指出總統現在的談判籌碼減少了，因為他無法再輕易利用這些關稅作為向其他國家施壓的手段。這暗示了法律挫敗直接削弱了他的外交談判能力。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 B 與文章內容相反；選項 D 過度推論，文章僅提到談判能力受限，並未說會完全接受對方要求。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does Jay Foreman mean when he describes the administration's approach as using a 'bazooka'?

options.A: The policy was excessively powerful and lacked precision.
options.B: The policy was designed to destroy foreign military equipment.
options.C: The policy was a secret weapon used to surprise business owners.
options.D: The policy was highly effective at targeting specific, strategic goods.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到 Foreman 認為對所有進口商品徵收 10% 關稅是錯誤的，並將此「廣泛的方法」比作「火箭筒（bazooka）」，對比他認為需要的「更小、更謹慎的工具」。這意味著該政策手段過於粗糙且缺乏針對性。選項 B 是字面解釋，不符合語境；選項 C 與原文無關；選項 D 與 Foreman 的觀點完全相反，他認為應該針對特定產品而非全面徵稅。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of the article regarding the Trump administration's trade policy?

options.A: The success of the administration in forcing companies to return to the U.S.
options.B: The process of how businesses can apply for tax refunds from the government.
options.C: The legal and strategic challenges facing the administration's tariff-based trade agenda.
options.D: The personal opinions of business owners regarding international trade laws.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文主要探討政府如何因關稅政策屢遭法院駁回而陷入困境，包括法律上的挫敗、對談判能力的影響，以及政府後續尋求新策略的嘗試。選項 A 與文意不符，因為政策受挫；選項 B 只是細節之一；選項 D 僅是文章的一部分，無法概括全文關於法律挑戰與貿易策略的核心主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
